# Sonic booms and diffusion wakes generated by a heavy quark in thermal AdS/CFT

> We evaluate the Poynting vector generated by a heavy quark moving through a thermal state of N=4 gauge theory using AdS/CFT. A significant diffusion wake is observed as well as a Mach cone. We discuss the ratio of the energy going into sound modes to the energy coming in from the wake.

## Key claim

Using a natural split of the energy flow into sonic and diffusive modes in the rest frame of the plasma, the sonic boom dissipates \(1+v^2\) times as much energy as the diffusion wake feeds in, but the authors caution that this ratio is derived from an essentially algebraic argument and should be regarded with some caution.

## Method

The authors compute the Poynting vector and energy density generated by an infinitely massive quark moving at constant velocity through a thermal state of \(\mathcal{N}=4\) super-Yang-Mills theory, using the AdS/CFT correspondence to translate metric perturbations of AdS\(_5\)-Schwarzschild into the expectation value of the boundary stress tensor. They split the Fourier-space stress tensor into analytical UV approximations (valid to order \(K^{-3}\)), analytical IR approximations (valid to order \(K^0\)), and a remainder that is uniformly bounded and integrable, then perform three-dimensional fast Fourier transforms on \(128^3\) grids with wave-numbers ranging from \(-10\) to \(10\) in each component, supplemented by finer one-dimensional FFTs for the IR terms with cubic denominators.

## Key numbers

- For \(v=0.75\), numerical results for the energy density and components of the Poynting vector are presented; \(|X|=1\) corresponds to \(0.2\,\text{fm}\) from the quark if \(T \approx 318\,\text{MeV}\).
- The energy loss into sound modes is greater than the total energy loss by a factor of \(1+1/v^2\), and the ratio of energy going into sound modes to energy fed in by the diffusion wake is \(1+v^2 : -1\) in the plasma rest frame.
- Conservation of the stress tensor was checked by comparing \(\dot{\mathcal{E}}\) to \(\nabla \cdot \vec{\mathcal{S}}\) in position space, and worked to within a few percent of \(\dot{\mathcal{E}}\) except when \(\dot{\mathcal{E}}\) became very small.

## Context

This work extends earlier AdS/CFT calculations of the stress tensor generated by a moving quark (Friess 2006, Yarom 2007, Gubser 2007, Chesler 2007) by providing sufficiently precise high-\(k\) estimates of \(\langle T^{0i}(\vec{k})\rangle\) and a robust numerical scheme for performing the Fourier transforms, enabling explicit extraction of the Poynting vector. The results enable quantitative comparison with RHIC phenomenology, where data from PHENIX (Adler 2005) favors suppression of the diffusion wake and STAR (Adams 2005) does not show as clear a minimum in associated particle production, while other authors (Casalderrey-Solana 2006) have suggested turning off the diffusion wake altogether in linearized hydro analysis and a model by Renk (2006) assumes 75% of energy lost goes into a sonic boom.
